content.problemStatement:
You are given two line segments AB\mathsf{AB}AB and CD\mathsf{CD}CD in NNN-dimensional space. Find the minimum distance between them, i.e. the minimum distance ‚ą£PQ‚ą£\mathsf{|PQ|}‚ą£PQ‚ą£ over all points P‚ąąAB\mathsf{P} \in \mathsf{AB}P‚ąąAB and Q‚ąąCD\mathsf{Q} \in \mathsf{CD}Q‚ąąCD.

**Note**: Because of large I/O, please use fast I/O methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč denoting the coordinates of the point A\mathsf{A}A.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bNb_1, b_2, \ldots, b_Nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bN‚Äč denoting the coordinates of the point B\mathsf{B}B.
The fourth line contains NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,‚Ä¶,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1‚Äč,c2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,cN‚Äč denoting the coordinates of the point C\mathsf{C}C.
The fifth line contains NNN space-separated integers d1,d2,‚Ä¶,dNd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_Nd1‚Äč,d2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,dN‚Äč denoting the coordinates of the point D\mathsf{D}D.


Output Format
For each query, print a single line containing one real number - the minimum distance between the line segments. Your output will be considered correct if the absolute or relative error does not exceed 10‚ąí1210^{-12}10‚ąí12.
constraints: 0‚Č§ai,bi,ci,di‚Č§1,0000 \le a_i, b_i, c_i, d_i \le 1,0000‚Č§ai‚Äč,bi‚Äč,ci‚Äč,di‚Äč‚Č§1,000 for each valid iii
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
